title: Unified AVL-tree forest architecture
description: pjson must use the same AVL-tree forest architectural paradigm as pmm for both memory management and stored object management. This eliminates functional and architectural duplication (e.g., the separate pool mechanism in the prototype).
rationale: Using a single architectural paradigm (AVL-tree forest) for both the memory manager and the stored objects reduces complexity, code duplication, and maintenance burden. The prototype's separate pool allocator introduces redundant functionality that pmm already provides.
author: netkeep80
acceptance_criteria: No separate pool allocator is used for JSON node management
JSON nodes are managed through pmm's AVL-tree-based allocation
Memory management and object storage share the same paradigm
